mapping.a_element: maximizing (relative) entropy subject to a set of constraints to fix the distribution
mapping.b_element: constructing the generalized Gibbs ensemble by maximizing entropy subject to the conserved-quantity constraints

mapping.a_element: the imposed moment constraints ⟨f_i⟩ = F_i on the distribution
mapping.b_element: the fixed expectation values of the model's conserved quantities (integrals of motion) that define the GGE

mapping.a_element: Lagrange multipliers λ_i conjugate to the moment constraints
mapping.b_element: the generalized inverse temperatures / Lagrange multipliers of the GGE conjugate to each conserved quantity
the GGE steady-state density matrix ρ ∝ exp(−Σ λ_i I_i)

brief_justification: Paper A derives the canonical exponential-family distribution obtained by maximizing (relative) entropy subject to moment constraints, and Paper B describes its post-quench steady state via a generalized Gibbs ensemble — which is exactly that same maximum-entropy-under-constraints construction, with the conserved-quantity expectation values playing the role of A's moments and the GGE's Lagrange multipliers playing the role of A's; the shared machinery is the MaxEnt/Gibbs variational principle. The rest of B's dynamical-mean-field-theory time evolution is not shared, but the statistical characterization of its endpoint maps role-for-role onto A's method.
